system.description:
Discipline: Neigong
Skill: Medium Melee against Evade
Type: Normal
Qi: 3

You thrust your staff towards a distant enemy and shoot a flowing beam of light in a color reminiscent of wine you have recently imbibed. Upon a successful strike, the target takes normal Damage from the staff and also becomes drunk, suffering -1d10 to Skills and Defenses (except Hardiness and Resolve which increase by 1) due to drunkenness for 1 hour. On a total success the target takes 1 Wound plus all other effects. The beam can travel 30 feet per Rank of Neigong. The user is drained of one Qi Rank which comes back at a rate of 1 per day and loses his inebriated state. Requirement: The user must be drunk to use this Technique. Cathartic: The user does not lose their inebriated state and recovers Qi Ranks at a rate of 1 per hour.
system.source: Wandering Heroes of Ogre Gate, Chapter 3: Kung Fu Techniques
system.targetDrains: skill.combat.armStrike=1; skill.combat.legStrike=1; skill.combat.grapple=1; skill.combat.throw=1; skill.combat.lightMelee=1; skill.combat.mediumMelee=1; skill.combat.heavyMelee=1; skill.combat.smallRanged=1; skill.combat.largeRanged=1
system.targetDrainsNote: Drunkenness imposes -1d10 to Skills and Defenses except Hardiness and Resolve increase by 1 for 1 hour. The structured buttons cover combat skill penalties; adjudicate other Skills and defense increases manually.
system.targetEffects: Target becomes drunk for 1 hour; staff beam range is 30 feet per Rank of Neigong
system.totalSuccessTargetEffects: Target becomes drunk for 1 hour and takes 1 direct Wound
system.consequenceNote: On a successful strike, the user is drained of one Qi Rank, recovered at 1 per day; Cathartic use recovers at 1 per hour.
system.requirementNotes: The user must be drunk to use this Technique.
system.catharticEffect: The user does not lose their inebriated state and recovers Qi Ranks at a rate of 1 per hour.
